data.title: Maintenance of Anaesthesia With Sevoflurane Versus Propofol and the Occurrence of Postoperative Headache.
data.brief_summary: Postoperative headache is a major issue after general anaesthesia and surgery. The aim of this study is to investigate the possible difference in the occurrence of postoperative headache when general anaesthesia is maintained with propofol, which is supposed to have protective effects on migraine patients, versus sevoflurane.
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* ASA I-III
* scheduled operation under general anaesthesia
* operations \<4 hours
* minorgynaecological operatios (leiomyomectomy)
* hernia repair
* arthroscopic procedurs
* minor orthopaedic operations without the use of tourniquet or cement
* saphenectomy
* minor urologic ope
endpoints: incidence of postoperative headache (yes or no)
